key_insight: V alloying turns MoTe2 into a magnetically active monolayer with intrinsic valley polarization that can be strongly tuned by electric field and biaxial strain.
engineering_relevance: The material offers a promising 2D platform for low-power valleytronic devices such as tunable transistors and sensors.
method: DFT calculations on substitutionally alloyed Mo0.75V0.25Te2 monolayer.
Stability checked via energetics and phonon calculations to confirm no imaginary modes.
Valley splitting evaluated under spin-orbit coupling, external electric fields, and biaxial strain.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports quantitative valley splittings up to 132.8 meV under electric field, 160.8 meV under tensile strain, and 54.4 meV under 2% compressive strain.
watch_item: The results are computational and the practical device performance will depend on synthesis control, defect tolerance, and experimental validation of the alloyed monolayer.
paper_title: Strain- and Electric-Field-Tunable Valley Polarization in Mo0.75V0.25Te2(Mo3VTe8) for Valleytronic Application
